headline: East Malaysia MPs Block 3 Constitutional Amendments
context: A bloc of 49 East Malaysian MPs blocked three proposed constitutional amendments in 2025 that would have reduced state powers over natural resources.

cards.big: 49 East Malaysian MPs blocked 3 constitutional amendments in 2025
cards.sub: Proposed changes would have shifted natural resource powers to federal agencies

cards.big: First blocked amendment would give Petronas direct leasing in state waters
cards.sub: Currently Petronas must negotiate production sharing with state petroleum agencies

cards.big: Second amendment proposed federal oversight of state forestry and logging permits
cards.sub: Sarawak Premier called it a direct attack on 60 years of timber sovereignty

cards.big: Two-thirds majority requires 148 votes but government mustered only 143
cards.sub: The bloc demonstrated constitutional veto power for the first time since MA63

cards.big: Veto power tests whether East Malaysian MPs can permanently protect state rights
Three constitutional amendments blocked by East Malaysian MPs show Borneo's parliamentary leverage is real.
